panels.title: What this dashboard shows in v0.1

options.content:
**v0.1 ships traces, not engine metrics.**

After the image-as-pod pivot, iplane is **not in the inference data path** — the operator's request goes laptop → provider proxy URL → engine, completely bypassing iplane. So the iplane self-metrics (`inference_requests_total`, latency histograms, etc.) that earlier drafts of this dashboard queried never tick under the current architecture.

Meanwhile vLLM's metrics live at the pod's `/metrics` endpoint (Prometheus scrape) — not on OTLP. Reaching them from a remote pod needs an otel-collector scrape config that knows the per-deploy proxy URL, which is v0.2 work (tracked as a follow-up issue).

**What works today:**

- **Engine traces** flow over OTLP via the cloudflared tunnel (or your hosted sink) and land in Tempo. Open **Explore → Tempo → Search** to see spans from your latest `iplane deployment query` / `make demo` calls.
- **The provider rate catalog** (panel below) shows the per-second $/hr cost iplane knows about for every provider in `providers.yaml`. Useful for the cost-economics chapter beat.

The iplane self-metric panels are deliberately removed pending v0.2's request-queue / scheduler, which will put iplane back into a position where its own metrics actually mean something.

panels.title: Provider rate catalog (from providers.yaml)
panels.description: Per-second cost for every provider iplane knows about, converted to $/hr for readability. This is the static catalog — what each provider WOULD charge if you provisioned on it. The cost-economics chapter beat (Ch 6 §6.7) reads this against trace volume / cold-start cost to inform 'where should I run this workload.'
